entry.title: Missing index file at DART/GDPDU extract
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I have introduced the 5XU Scope item for document reporting and compliance at our customer in the public cloud and can therefore also make the Dart extract in the app run statutory reports. we have now handed this over to the customer and he has given it to the auditor to check whether it can be accepted. Here the comment came up that the index file is missing, which the auditor still needs in any case.

Is there a way to activate this by default in the public cloud so that it is also spit out during generation?

Many thanks and best regards,
Vanessa

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...

entry.title: Unable to create contact in Fiori App Manage Customer Master Data
entry.content:
Hello,

in Fiori App Manage Customer Master Data, button create doesn't appear.

I have all the autorization mentionned in Fiori App IMPLEMENTATION INFORMATION (Found nothing missing in SU53).

Also I activated oData Service MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01.

I can maintened my Partner, I can even edit or delete a contact, but not create a new one.

We could before the system upgrade.

Do you know what I could be missing ?

Thanks in advance,

Best regards.

entry.title: Cloud ALM - Business Service Management strategy
entry.content:
Hi,

We are implementing Cloud ALM for central monitoring (one CALM for all businesses) for multiple businesses. Each business has 4 tier landscape and multiple ECC, S/4 instances. So what is the recommendation for BSM in this case. Is it best to group in a way like, business wise, or Lower landscape and Production wise (like Dev will be on BSM group and PRD will be another).  

Also, SAP cloud services perspective, how that can be mapped once it is grouped into business / lower environment specific? (OR All SAP cloud services mapped separately into another BSM ?) 

Kindly provide your input. What is the best recommendation from SAP in this case. 

Thanks,
Raja

entry.title: SAP University Alliances Global Knowledge Sharing Session
entry.content:
Join our Quarterly Webinars to help you stay connected with the latest teaching and learning opportunities from the SAP University Alliances program.

The webinar is composed of 40-45 minutes of presentation and sharing, followed by 10-15 minutes of Q&A. There are two time slots available for each session. One fits more for EMEA & APAC, and another fits more for AMER & EMEA. When registering, please also leave any questions so we will be able to address them properly in the webinar later. 

 

Agenda of the sessions on July 1, 2025

  • Welcome and news from SAP University Alliances
  • News from SAP
  • Deep dive to SAP Learning Hub, student edition
  • Intro for new SAP UA members
  • Q&A

 

Register for the session (EMEA & APAC) on 10AM, CET, Tuesday, July 1

Register for the session (AMER & EMEA) on 5PM, CET, Tuesday, July 1

 

Feel free to leave comments or suggestions here.

entry.title: Validation Check for Driver % Summing to 100% by Cost Center in SAC Table via Script
entry.content:
Hi All,

I’m working on a planning scenario in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and need help implementing a validation check on a table.

The table contains driver values that are expected to sum up to 100%, but the key requirement is that this summation must be validated with respect to each cost center, even when the table includes multiple combinations of other dimensions.

Currently, I’ve enabled the "Totals" row in the table, which allows for visual verification, but this doesn’t serve as an actual validation. What I’m looking for is:

A way to check programmatically or through scripting if the driver percentages sum to 100% for each cost center

If the sum is not 100% for any cost center, a popup message or alert should display, identifying which cost centers are affected

     Budget
     2024
     Driver
CostcenterCountryChannelBrandProduct 
CC1Totals   1
CC1#2##0.15
CC1#3##0.75
CC1#4##0.1

Is there any way to achieve this through scripting (e.g., in an analytic application) or through any other built-in SAC functionality?

Any guidance or examples would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: SAP DM: Example View Plugin not visible in POD Designer
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have done the steps that are available in the Example View Plugin documentation (https://help.sap.com/docs/sap-digital-manufacturing/pod-plugin-developer-s-guide/view-pod-plugin-template-and-example?locale=en-US). The plugin is deployed, running in BTP and also registered in Service Registry in DM. It is visible in the Production Process Designer but not in the POD Designer. The POD type was enabled in the components in BAS. Do I need to enable anything else in DM? Does anyone know how to fix this?

Thanks!

entry.title: Re: S/4Hana 2023 - Key User Extensibility - Custom Logic App - Keep the Core Clean

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to automatically run the query in SAP...
entry.content:
You can use Python (from a file or a Jupyter Notebook) to connect to the SAP Datasphere HANA Database. This allows you to send queries, retrieve data, and use it as you wish (e.g., store it in a file, convert it to Excel, send it to a database).

To configure SQL access, you'll need to do it space by space within Space Management (see Integrating Data via Database Users/Open SQL Schemas | SAP Help Portal). 

However, if you're asking about using Python Transformation within a Data Flow in SAP Datasphere, then no, that's not possible. Input/output functions are prohibited for Python code executed in DSP Data Flows.

Regads,

entry.title: How to do COGS splitting for the GL accounts(non-COGS) as per CCS in I/C STO scenario for IC AP
entry.content:
How to do COGS splitting for the GL accounts(non-COGS) as per CCS in I/C STO scenario for IC AP.

We want to split the GL account(CE category as 1) which is being used in intercompany AP posting as COGS I/C account(not maintained in OBYC i.e. not true COGS account) as per cost component structure.

The entry here is

COGS I/C...Dr(this needs to be split)

   Vendor...Cr

this is posted with document type KR and transaction code FB01(through IDOC).

Has anyone tried to split normal GL account as for COGS splitting?

Thanks,

Praphulla

entry.title: 発注伝票納入先住所について
entry.content:

※詳細は添付ファイル「【問い合わせ資料】発注伝票納入先住所について.xlsx」をご参照ください。

Hello Community,

We are working with a possible SAP customer that needs to manage IFRS 15 accounting principle related to projects.

Can you please let us know where to find information about:

  • Coverage of IFRS 15 for OP solution
  • Further products need to use this solution

Thanks,

Marco

entry.title: Strategy Group 52 in SAP IBP
entry.content:
In SAP IBP standard, we can execute a process such as Strategy Group 40 (MTS; Make-to-Stock), which takes us from Forecast to Demand Plan to Supply Run. This results in Planned Orders in IBP, which are then sent to S/4 and converted into Production and Procurement Orders without requiring a Sales Order. The stock is therefore not customer-specific, and when a Sales Order arrives, it is allocated from this general inventory.

Similarly, Strategy Group 81 (MTO; Make-to-Order) in S/4 is triggered only after a Sales Order with account assignment, creating a Planned Order that is linked to the Sales Order. This process creates customer-specific stock only, without shared stock.

Some customers, however, would like to anticipate component planning even before a Sales Order arrives. In S/4, we could use Strategy Group 52 (Hybrid MTS), where the forecast creates non-convertible planned orders as anonymous stock (not tied to a specific customer). Once a Sales Order arrives, MRP replaces the forecast with sales-specific planned orders, functioning like pseudo-MTO, but without customer-specific stock. This allows for early component and capacity planning while maintaining flexibility before the sales order is confirmed.

The question is: how would we implement Strategy Group 52 when planning the Finished Product in SAP IBP? Would it make sense to use Strategy Group 81 with a workaround—by creating non-convertible Planned Orders (using the Conversion Indicator flag)—so that no Production Orders are created before a Sales Order arrives, while still enabling early material (component) requirements planning and capacity planning? Or are there alternative approaches, experiences, or customizations in SAP IBP that simulate the behavior of Strategy Group 52?

sc

entry.title: Re: SAP IDM: check field value before submit a form
entry.content: You may want to check Rhidmo, an open source implementation of SAP IDM Extension Framework which requires Java deployment in SAP NetWeaver only once (for the initial setup) and then allows to implement your onSubmit and onLoad functions as normal package scripts in SAP IDM Developer Studio.

entry.title: Re: Error during request to remote service: Request faile...
entry.content:
@kaizen9 

Have you specified the credentials.path parameter in the package.json which points to the relative service path, as shown in the example below?

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/using-services#btp-destinations

"cds": {
  "requires": {
    "API_BUSINESS_PARTNER": {
      "kind": "odata",
      "model": "srv/external/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER",
      "[production]": {
        "credentials": {
          "destination": "S4HANA",
          "path": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER"
        }
      }
    }
  }
}

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...
entry.content:
Hello,

One reason is that when you select a sales order item, there could be multiple schedule lines relevant. Some schedule lines are editable and some are not with delivery block. In such case, please go to tab "Schedule Lines" to update delivery block. 

Best regards,

Yong

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP Odata V2 and V4 create save data.
entry.content:
@Abhinandan_Dutt 
The section "Invalidating State Messages" of the RAP document explains why that piece of code is required. In a nutshell, it is for invalidating (clearing) previous state messages. Without it, duplicate error messages will appear on the UI.

entry.title: BYD2505 Enable Inventory Valuation Configuration
entry.content:
We would like to know that there is any way to calculate inventory valuation per Identified Stock. Please instruct me if it's enable in configuration or customization.

As far as we know we can enable Inventory Valuation Configuration including Valuation per Identified Stock, right?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: BYD2505 Enable Inventory Valuation Configuration
entry.content:
We would like to clarify that in SAP Business ByDesign, inventory valuation is not directly supported at the level of individual Identified Stocks. 

However, there is an important relationship between Identified Stock and Valuation Level:

As documented in the SAP Help Portal (https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_BUSINESS_BYDESIGN/2754875d2d2a403f95e58a41a9c7d6de/2cd3b1dd722d10149df5a1d1175a4035.html?q=Identified+Stock) , the valuation level for a material is determined at the point when the first Identified Stock is activated for a given product-specification combination.

If the product is set to be valued at Product Specification level and you activate an Identified Stock tied to that specification, all stock under that specification will be valued accordingly.

Even if you later change the valuation level in the material master, Identified Stocks created afterward will inherit valuation based on the current setting only if no previous Identified Stock exists for that product-specification.

Therefore, valuation follows the product-specification group, not each Identified Stock individually.

Conclusion:

There is no standard configuration or scoping question to enable valuation per Identified Stock directly.

The valuation is per product-specification or site, and Identified Stock is used for logistics traceability, not financial valuation.

Customization for per-Identified Stock valuation is not supported in standard system behavior.

entry.title: User has posted GRN without creating inbound delivery?
entry.content:
I checked below points.

1. In vendor master confirmation control key (CCK) is maintained.

2. In info-record CCK is maintained.

3. in PO, for specific line item CCK is maintained.

4. Configuration of CCK is also perfect.

5. This particular behaviour is happening for only two line item(which has same material) in that PO which contains 300 line item. I referred the different line item but didn't find any difference.

If I am missing anything please let me know.

entry.title: Re: New Problem in for change Previous Period in MMPI T C...
entry.content: Yes, This configuration worked. first i deactivated material Ledger then i changed period from T Code MMPI (for update previous period). now it's configured for previous period. and then again i activated.

entry.title: How to Disable Automatic Payment Allocation?
entry.content:
Is there any option to disable automatic payment allocation when a manual bank statement is posted?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: How to Disable Automatic Payment Allocation?
entry.content:
The Business Configuration activity ''Settings for Posting Automatically Generated Bank Statement'' impact the automation of bank statements postings.

  • If all columns (Company, Bank Country, Bank ID, Currency and Bank Account ID) are marked as ''*'', then the automatic posting of a bank statement will not take place.
    After uploading the bank statement, the status of the same will stay as ''In Preparation'' and the automatic posting will not be triggered.
  • If values are maintained in at least one column, then the bank statement will be posted automatically if no errors are encountered.

entry.title: Re: useOneAsMany leaving out one node in target
entry.content:
Most likely, you need to set the mapping context properly to get your mapping fixed.

Here we have an example where we explain how to set the mapping context:

https://help.sap.com/docs/integration-suite/sap-integration-suite/set-mapping-context

Hope this helps

Alex

entry.title: Re: Retention Amount in Sales Order Showing with VAT in M...
entry.content:
Hello,

Excluding VAT from Retention Line

Based on SAP S/4HANA configurations for managing VAT and retention amounts, it is possible to configure VAT handling so that retention amounts are excluded.

  1. Tax Handling Configuration: Processing VAT on Retention You can specify whether to apply VAT to retention documents or not. This configuration is performed per country/region and requires checking the ‘Tax Reduction’ checkbox in relevant settings.
  2. Manual Entries: Manual correction might be needed if automatic processes do not accurately assign VAT as per custom needs.

Separate Reporting and VAT Calculation

  • Configuration Entries: Ensure proper configuration in the ‘Tax Handling for Retention’ and related activities, such as maintaining tax codes and settings that differentiate VAT treatment for retention documents.
  • Custom Reporting: Generate reports separately to ensure retention amounts and relevant VAT are correctly displayed or omitted as necessary.

Best regards, Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: the APP mass change of sales order can't set delivery...
entry.content: @zhaoyo, Thanks.

entry.title: Background Creation of Production versions
entry.content:
As we all know, production versions are mandatory in S/4 Hana. In our line of business, they were never necessary because all the decisions in terms of BOM and plan selection could be achieved through Customizing. 
I have problems seeing the plus side of PVs. Now we need to maintain one element more than before to maintain the same functionality. That's no additional value. Even phantom assemblies need to have a production version, and we easily have about 100 of them in a multi-level BOM. We are looking to automate the creation of PVs as much as possible to avoid redundant work for the users.
Here's what I found but I would like a smoother solution.
Most of our requirements are fulfilled by the program CS_BOM_PRODVER_MIGRATION02 that can simply run in a background job at night in various steps for the needed combinations of BOM usages and routing counters in descending priority. This program even creates the PVs for phantom parts without routings. However, it works only for BOMs and routing whose valid-from date is not in the future. And we would like to create PVs right away for parts that will be active in the future, so that their planned orders will generate requirements early enough for Purchasing to start sourcing.
The Fiori app F6400 has a nice surface. It shows PV proposals that will be created whenever a BOM or routing is saved (if you activate the event linkage). The proposals need to be confirmed manually but you can only select 20 PVs at a time to approve. Unfortunately, this app does not seem to work too well. I also haven't found settings yet to make it create proposals for "BOM-only" PVs. It would however create PVs whose validity starts in the future. On the other hand, the rules you can define in the Customizing for the creation of the proposals do not include important settings like priorities for BOM usages or routing usages. 
Neither of the two programs will create a PV with reference to "routing-only". I admit this is a rare business case but when you create a PV manually it is a valid option that will create a green traffic light, so why shouldn't these programs be able to do that?
I don't like the idea of running a mixed approach between the Fiori app and the background job and still not having all requirements covered. Did someone find a better solution to organize the automatic creation? Please don't propose LSMW or BDC...
Regards,
Dana

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!
entry.content:
Hello @Anibal_Ruiz - welcome to SAP Community. We have a lot of SAP Champions in EMEA - including a few based in Spain - you could try and connect with some of those. entry.title: Re: Document Due Notification
entry.content: Thanks Mridul, So suppose there are four steps: the first step owner is the employee, the second step owner is the manager, the third step owner is the upline manager, and the fourth step owner is HR. If the employee has not yet submitted the form to the next step, the employee will receive a notification. Similarly, whoever is holding the form will receive a document due notification based on the due date set in the form template settings.

Where do I log a ticket? thank you.

entry.title: Extending Efficiency: Automating IBP RTI Connectivity steps with SAP CIAS
entry.content:
The SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP) release in late 2023 marked a significant milestone by providing real-time integration connectivity. This breakthrough offered substantial benefits for both customers and partners, simplifying integration processes, reducing errors, and accelerating deployments. If you are new to CIAS in context of SAP IBP Real-Time integration setup, please check out this article: Fast track your SAP IBP Real time integration journey 

What's new?

With the release of IBP 2505, the existing solution has moved even further, bringing automation to the forefront of integration tasks. Most IBP setup steps have now been automated, thanks to CIAS, significantly reducing manual work and the potential for human errors.

Key Automations with IBP 2505

Here's a summary of what's been automated with the latest IBP 2505 via CIAS:

Connectivity Setup – IBP Inbound Integration:

  • SAP_COM_0769 and SAP_COM_0770: These integrations have seen a full automation overhaul. The setup steps involved in establishing inbound connectivity are now seamlessly handled by CIAS.
  • Communication User and Communication System Creation: The automation process includes creating new communication users and systems, ensuring these essentials are efficiently managed without manual intervention.
  • Communication Arrangement Creation: Quickly establish communication arrangements with full automation, reducing configuration time significantly.

Connectivity Setup – IBP Outbound Integration:

  • SAP_COM_0684 and SAP_COM_0685: These integrations also benefit from CIAS's automated setup, streamlining outbound connectivity processes.
  • Communication Systems and Arrangements: Similar automations in creating necessary communication systems and arrangements are found here, allowing for swift and precise configuration.

Please be aware the Communication System for SAP_COM_0684 requires one manual step currently! Follow the Automation Instructions carefully during this step, maintaining efficiency and accuracy while leveraging CIAS's automated capabilities.

Prerequisites

The SAP_COM_0A31 communication scenario, introduced with IBP 2505, is a prerequisite for utilizing automation steps in IBP Cloud system. CIAS offers detailed manual instructions for activating this scenario seamlessly as part of the workflow.

How to run automated steps?

Workflow includes both manual steps and steps that can be executed as automated steps. You can easily recognize if there is automation for a step, you'll see:

  • "Automation Instructions" chapter appears for the step
  • "Execute Task" button appears beside "Task Completed" button

Follow these steps to execute automation effectively:

  • Enter mandatory parameters: Fill in any mandatory parameters that may be required.
  • Execute Automation: Click the "Execute Task" button.
  • Initial Setup for New IBP Systems, if it's the first time executing automation for a specific IBP system:

 

  • Navigate to the System Access tab
  • Click the "Setup" button on the right.

  • Follow manual instructions to configure SAP_COM_0A31. In each screen you can use the navigation link to open the application where you have to perform the steps.

  • Re-execute Task: Click the "Execute Task" button again.

  • Monitor Execution Status: The status icon will change content and color during execution:
    • To be Executed
    • In Process
    • Success or Error

  • Error Handling: If an error occurs, check the execution log for details

  • Complete the workflow by clicking on the "Task Completed" button and confirm to move forward to the next Step
  • If you click on the "Show/Hide" button you can display the technical parameters that the workflow uses as input from the previous steps. These fields are always read only fields.

These steps ensure a smooth integration process, leveraging CIAS's automation capabilities and manual instructions when necessary.

Conclusion: Enhancing Integration Efficiency

SAP's CIAS is a robust tool that empowers businesses by delivering comprehensive automation in cloud integration tasks, speeding up the system configuration process, and significantly reducing the likelihood of errors. 

For SAP customers and partners, these advancements symbolize a crucial step forward in optimizing integration workflows, offering a newfound ease and efficiency in navigating the complex landscape of cloud-based integrations.

entry.title: Story Report Output Issue
entry.content:
Hi,

I have created the story report and it is working fine for me even if I am proxying anyone else, but if the same report is run by other users Performance Rating appears in decimal and column names also appearing differently in some cases.

And in few cases output is blank but once I proxied, I got the output.

Kindly suggest.

Regards,
P

entry.title: Re: Vendor Batch and Best-Before Date in EWM
entry.content: Not sure in your EWM, maybe it is different for each system. But in our production EWM, Vendor Batch is stored in table AUSP. Filter the OBJEK field with the material key and batch combined as string. Use the field ATINN further to filter only Vendor Batch with constant value "LOBM_LICHA".

entry.title: Re: Allocation of Budget - different legal entities on 1...
entry.content:
Hi @xavierlegarrec,

Many thanks for your feedback and the recommendation.
I found in one KBA the following bullet point for using the option "based on Groups" for the budgeting.


The link to this source is: 2084214 - Budget - Based on Groups - Compensation

Furthermore, SAP recommends following approach: 

So, my follow-up question would be, if you would recommend to use for every entity a separate template in order to avoid mixing up the budgets of the different guidelines. I think, the advantages of doing this approach outweigh the disadvantages.

Thank you in advance for your reply.


Best Regards,
Joachim

entry.title: Data source extension for outgoing checks
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

Are there available data source extension for outgoing checks triggered from the Manage Automatic Payments app and generated in the Manage Outgoing Checks app. 

I am using SAP S/4Hana Public Cloud edition system. 

Thank You 

Regards, 

Melle

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Extensibility 

#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Nagaraj J

entry.title: Re: How to fetch Country of Origin from MARA to MCH1 in b...
entry.content:
You should be able to create a configuration profile (CU41) with batch classification enabled, as well as a procedure (CU01) to copy the necessary characteristic values from the material assigned to the configuration profile. Have you tried this yet? Did you get stuck?

Note: as a former ABAP developer, I used BADIs such as BADI_BATCH_CLASSIFICATION or BADI_OBJECTS_BATCH, rather than your preferred option.

entry.title: Carry-forward time valuation results into next day
entry.content:
Hi,

 

Our customer wishes to handle night work on the day it is actually worked.  So when a user clocks in from 10 PM to 6 AM, we want 6h to be paid on the next day.

I was hoping to achieve this by following the logic for rest rule validation, i.e. fill a time container on day D and use this to generate a time record on day D+1 .  However, when trying to use this new record to generate a time pay type on day D+1 I keep running into the error that it can only be used to "produce alerts or a flexible-period time container type".

Is it possible at all to transfer time from a time type group to the next day, and use it for payroll ?

 

Note: We're not looking to generate a different time pay type after 12 AM.  The customer wants the time valuation output to be dated to the next day.

 

Thank you in advance,

Kind regards,

Raf

 

@Volker_Ruof

@NeeleshKamath

entry.title: Internet facing Fiori application
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

The client has S4HANA public cloud with SAP Success Factors and without Cloud Identity Services. My task is to provide access to SAP Workzone with a custom Fiori application for guests to enter personal details from outside the company network without having to create a SAP user for each of them and then save the information in SF. I see the concept of External users in SAP SF, however, I'm not sure if it works without a user for each individual.

Appreciate your thoughts and assistance.

Thanks and Best Regards,

Rajat

entry.title: Multiple Table View on Object Pages in CAPM
entry.content:
Hello Experts!

I needed to render multiple views on a table in an object page. I understand this is usually doable in list report pages and also referring to link (https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blog-posts-by-sap/cap-with-fiori-elements-configure-multiple-views-on-list-report-tables/ba-p/13579909) for the same. Is there a way the same feature can be developed in objects pages too?

Thank you!

entry.title: 発注伝票納入先住所について Regarding the delivery address on the order slip
entry.content:
仕入先直送受注伝票ヘッダの「出荷先コード」を変更し、同時に変更後の出荷先の「住所」も変更された後、発注明細「50」のみの「納入先住所コード」が空白の状態となっていました。
※詳細は添付ファイル「【問い合わせ資料】発注伝票納入先住所について.xlsx」をご参照ください。
The "shipping destination code" in the header of the direct order invoice from the supplier was changed, and at the same time, the address of the changed shipping destination was also updated. However, the "delivery address code" in the order detail "50" remained blank. *For more details, please refer to the attached file "[Inquiry Material] About Delivery Address of Order Invoice.xlsx."
上記の状況はSAP標準機能の不具合のようので、根本原因についてご確認いただけますと幸いです。
The above situation seems to be a bug in the SAP standard functionality, so I would appreciate it if you could check on the root cause.

entry.title: Re: Hide the controller file while inspecting in browser for sapui5
entry.content: You exclude the map files from deployment and all others dbg files so only minified code is visible. Make debugging in the frontend very difficult! Why want do you "hide" in the first place?

entry.title: Setup: Only send payment advice via mail if supplier was paid more than 4 invoices
entry.content:
How to configurate sending payment advices via email only after the supplier was paid more then 4 invoices. If equal to 4 or less, no sending of the payment advice is required.

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: SAP MaxAttention IWS 'Transform your Business with SAP Business AI' (Virtual, June 10, 2025)
entry.content:
Virtual 

June 10, 2025

Agenda

Dear Valued Customer,

We are pleased to invite you to the SAP MaxAttention Innovation Workshop focusing on ‘Transform your Business with SAP Business AI – From innovation to impact’ taking place virtually on June 10th, 2025. Discover how SAP Business AI can deliver transformative value across business processes by breaking down functional silos and fueling cross-enterprise collaboration. Please find the current agenda here.

In this workshop, your will understand various aspects of leveraging SAP Business AI to drive innovation and achieve impactful business outcomes. Through engaging keynotes, customer success stories, and expert-led discussions, attendees will gain a comprehensive understanding of SAP’s AI strategy and its practical applications:

  • Learn about the transformative potential of SAP Business AI from industry leaders and discover how it can revolutionize enterprise data management and AI, empowering leaders to make more impactful decisions.
  • Hear firsthand experiences from customers using SAP Business AI across finance, supply chain, customer experience, and HR, highlighting how these solutions enhance efficiency and drive success.
  • Gain insights into SAP MaxAttention’s role in accelerating AI adoption, including best practices and key learnings from successful AI projects.
  • Learn about the evolution of Joule, the rise of Agentic AI, and the role of Knowledge Graphs in enriching AI agents with enterprise context for higher-quality outcomes. Experience the latest embedded AI innovations within the SAP Business Suite and understand their real-world business impact
  • Explore how AI-driven techniques facilitate custom code migration during SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) transformations. Understand how AI-enhanced tools such as SAP LeanIX, SAP Signavio, SAP Cloud ALM, and Tricentis optimize business processes, support application lifecycle management, and accelerate testing.

Your will walk away with a clear understanding of how SAP Business AI can transform their business operations, drive efficiency, and enhance decision-making. SAP MaxAttention will support your customers throughout their AI journey, from initial planning to full deployment, bridging the gap from AI Experimentation to AI Value realization.

Our Workshops

These sessions and workshop series focus on a specific topic and the purpose is to enable our customers to understand the product portfolio and innovative technologies of SAP in order to drive adoption and consumption. As specifically now, it is more important than ever to help companies to be successful and to respond to changing markets by transforming into intelligent enterprises in the experience economy.

Important

Please note that the 'Attend' button which can be found within this blog post does not serve as a formal registration for the event.

To officially register, please reach out to me, paul.schropp@sap.com and I will ensure that your registration is properly processed.

We look forward to having you join us for our upcoming events and thank you for your understanding!

Your feedback

Feel free to share your feedback and thoughts in the comment section below.

entry.title: Re: Working with RAP BO Events end-to-end - Event-driven-architecture - Event framework
entry.content:
Hi Mlauber,

I have posted one issue here regarding local event consumption. Can you please take a look and help

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/sap-rap-events-local-consumption/qaq-p/14111252

Regards,

Ashish Pandey

entry.title: Re: Cost Center Hierarchy consolidation and replication
entry.content: Based on some discussions with SAP , I learnt that Cost Center Hierarchy is not part of sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter. But additional fields can be extended to input/receive Hierarchy node information from Source. Which means also on Source S/4H and S/4C a custom BAPI needs to be created to map to the custom field created in MDI.

entry.title: Re: ✨ How to Identify SAP Fiori Apps Based on RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model)
entry.content:
Hello @Marian_Zeis 

Thank you for sharing your perspective and taking the time to read the blog.

RAP supports both OData V2 & V4, but some features and many UI capability is supported in OData V4 only. So if the service is OData V4 it is "Strong  Indicator" that it could be RAP based FIORI Application.

Metadata can be used in to create a RAP based FIORI app.

The approach you mentioned to search for Service Defination in ADT. Indeed that will give me the RAP structure. But not everytime you know the Service Defination Name ??

Then even Functional or Process Owner would give you the FIORI app ID or App name then to know how it has been build you can try the above parameters / Filters.

As mentioned in the Blog, SAP Doesnot provide a direct filter to identify a RAP based FIORI app, but the process mentioned in the blog should help in that case.

I would happy to learn a better and easier approach.

entry.title: Customer & Partner Roundtable for SAP BTP ABAP Environment #24
entry.content:
👉 The call for contributions for one of the upcoming roundtables is open! 

 

If you want to show a demo or share a use case scenario for SAP BTP ABAP Environment send us an email and we will get back to you.
 

Introduction

 

As SAP BTP ABAP environment (aka Steampunk) and ABAP Cloud became more popular inside and outside of SAP, there is a high demand for rolling out the latest product news and updates, asking questions, and showing demos. 

💡 You can find the slides presented, recordings, and further references from the previous roundtables in this GitHub repository.


Meeting Information

When:                                                                      
  • June 25th, 10:00 - 11:00 AM CET  – Zoom Meeting (please register in advance) 
 
Who:
  • All interested customers, partners, and stakeholders are invited to join this roundtable
  • BTP ABAP team:
    • Frank Jentsch (Product Lead for SAP BTP ABAP Environment)
    • Iwona Hahn (BTP ABAP Product Management)

  • Partner Softway AG: Frank Engert (CEO)

Preliminary Agenda:
  • Product update for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
  • "Global E-Invoice Cloud" - managed SaaS solution on SAP BTP ABAP Environment: live demo, subaccount handling, customer onboarding, system connections
  • Q&A 
Looking forward to meeting you!
 


Check out our SAP Business Technology ABAP Environment page in SAP Community for product updates and upcoming events.

entry.title: Re: sap ehs incident management task program
entry.content:
Dear Rej1,

the two reports you mentioned are only required if you migrate/upgrade from S/4HANA 1709 (S4CORE 102) or below to one of the more recent versions. This also applies to an upgrade from the Component Extension for SAP EHS Management (EHSM, Add-on) to any S/4HANA version of 1809 or later.

If you do not migrate from any of the mentioned versions, the report is not required - as there won't be any data in terms of workflows specific to EHS to migrate. However, if you are running an upgrade, the two reports will be required. It is crucial to have a look at the SAP Note 2612110 - S4TWL - Renovation of task management before the upgrade, as there might be specific changes required due to custom adaptions of the workflows in EHS.

With kind regards,

Michael

entry.title: PLM Tech Uncovered: SAP Integrated Product Development Update May 2025
entry.content:
Are you up to hear about the newest features of SAP Integrated Product Development?

May 2025 brings the following highlights for your business:

Change Management

  • New action added for specifications
    New Trigger Workflow action is added for specification in the Manage Statuses app.

Specification Management

  • Compare composition in specifications
    You can now compare specifications-based composition data in the Compare Specifications app.

Visualization

  • Generate hotspots using AI for PDF documents
    You can now generate automatic hotspots using AI for PDF documents. You can perform this action by using the new icon when editing a PDF in the Hotspot Authoring screen.

Engineering

  • Change history for requirements
    You can view the change history for the requirements and download the change history.

To stay informed until the next update you can:

  • Subscribe to the What’s New Viewer on SAP Integrated Product Development Help Portal https://url.sap/5nbm4a
  • Join our LinkedIn group SAP Solutions for PLM https://www.linkedin.com/groups/9257139/
  • Visit us at www.sap.com/IPD

Rajesh

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #6) Document Grounding in Joule
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

 

In this blog, we will dive deep into Document Grounding in Joule.

 

Let's First Understand What Document Grounding is

Let’s understand this with an example.

If you ask Joule a question which relies on a company's internal information, for example:

“Can I use my private car for my business trip?”

You probably know the answer is in a policy documents, but don’t want to go through documents but just get answer using Joule.

This is exactly what Joule’s document grounding capability can do.

If Joule response this in usual way, by leveraging a LLM, then the response will be a generic one based on general SAP knowledge.

However....

If we feed Joule with company’s specific documents (like internal policies, reports and documents), then Joule can use “document grounding” and give more accurate and relevant answers.

 

So, what exactly is document grounding?

Document grounding is the process of making a LLM refer to specific documents to generate more accurate and relevant responses.

Instead of relying only on its pre-trained knowledge, the LLM pulls information from external sources to answer questions with real, up-to-date data.

 

Document Grounding in Joule

The document grounding capability of Joule helps us:

  • Upload documents in SAP and third-party repositories
  • And help Joule provide more comprehensive responses using information gathered from those documents

 

How does Document Grounding in Joule work?

Below image shows how document grounding works in Joule.

 

First customers need to setup the document grounding in Joule:

  1. Customers connect their document repositories via their own Microsoft SharePoint.
  2. Document Grounding AI Service is setup in SAP BTP.
  3. The Document Grounding AI Service takes the relevant customer-owned documents via Microsoft SharePoint and creates so called, vector embeddings.
  4. The vector embeddings are stored in SAP HANA Cloud Vector Engine.

 

Once set up, this process runs once per day to ensure the latest document contents are always available to Joule.

When users can ask a query to Joule and flows work as below:

  1. User enter prompt in Joule UI and its send to Joule service on SAP BTP.
  2. Joule’s dialog manager sends the user question/prompt to its internal RAG service.
  3. The RAG service creates a vector “embedding” of the prompt. The vector embedding in simple words is - the vector of numbers that represents the question. The vector embedding is further used to perform a “vector query” against SAP HANA Vector Engine. The vector query is a kind of mathematical similarity search that returns sections of documents that are relevant to the users question. In this example, it might be sections from an employee manual detailing allowable trip expenses and reimbursement rates.
  4. SAP HANA Vector Engine returns the relevant document extract.
  5. The RAG service passes the relevant documents extracts back to the Joule Dialog Manager along with information about the source documents.
  6. The Dialog Manager appends the user question with the relevant extracts and sends the “enriched” prompt to the LLM.
  7. The LLM provide the response, “grounded” with the company-specific data and also appends links to the sources of the enrichment data.
  8. The Dialog Manager further sends the response along with links to sources, back to the Joule UI. The user gets a concise, reliable, natural language answer. They can optionally explore the links for quick verification and more details.

 

Current Scope of the document grounding capability in Joule

  • Data repository: Microsoft SharePoint
  • File format: PDF and Microsoft Word
  • Document content: Plain text (Tables and images are not supported)
  • Language: English
  • Content refresh: SharePoint content updated once every day

 

This is in nutshell - Document Grounding in Joule!

 

What's Next?

Blog #7 - Joule for Developers [Will be published soon]

Meanwhile, you may read the blog Joule, SAP’s Generative AI Copilot

entry.title: Simplify the downtime-optimized System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA with Customizing on Temp Instance
entry.content:
---

TL;DR 

Software Update Manager’s downtime-optimized conversion approach significantly reduces downtime during a system conversion to SAP S/4HANA. This approach is now significantly improved, resulting in fewer conversion cycles which means reducing the overall project runtime.

The key idea here is making the required Customizing settings on a temporary instance. This approach allows you to adjust the required Customizing settings during a downtime-optimized conversion run, therefore eliminating the need for additional standard conversion cycles. 

---

 

If you want to convert your system to SAP S/4HANA (also sometime referred to as brownfield approach), you're talking Software Update Manager. This tool is well-known to SAP Basis and Technology folks as it is the standard tooling for SAP system maintenance.

Software Update Manager also covers the important conversion step from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA. If you’re familiar with system conversions, or are already in the planning phase, you know by now that it is NOT just another upgrade. It is not only a technical step to enable the system to consume latest technologies (AI is calling!), but also requires thorough preparation at application level. And in addition, using Software Update Manger for the conversion might result in a longer downtime than your business is willing to accept. 

 

Recap of downtime-optimized Conversion

But here's the amazing news: There is a downtime-optimized approach available with Software Update Manager that moves activities that normally require the business to be stopped, into an uptime window in which the Software Update Manager works in parallel to the ongoing business.

More information on this approach called downtime-optimized conversion is available here.

The magic of this approach, in addition to utilizing the capabilities of downtime-optimized Database Migration Option (DMO) - lies in moving data conversion activities to uptime. This even includes activities which normally have to be executed manually after the conversion with Software Update Manager is complete. This reduces downtime significantly. The image below illustrates the standard conversion, the block FIN (Finance) & ML (Material Ledger) Data Conversion represents the manual activities.

 

 With a downtime-optimized conversion, the process looks like this:

 

The big downside...

HOWEVER, there is one major challenge: To automate the manual FIN&ML conversion activities, Software Update Manager requires information on what to do. To provide this information, a standard conversion run is required that also includes the manual FIN&ML conversion. This is typically done on a copy of the production system. The resulting Customizing settings are saved in a customer transport, which is then included in the next downtime-optimized Software Update Manager run.

Doesn't sound like a challenge? Well - that's true. But the Knackpunkt (excuse my German, crucial point simply doesn't sound dramatic enough 🤔) arises with time. As the initial standard conversion that runs on a copy of production and the actual production go-live are often some months apart, the Customizing on the production system very likely changes in the course of time.

And the result ⁉️

The content of the initial Customizing transport and the actual existing Customizing in the production system no longer match. If you were to run a downtime-optimized conversion using said Customizing transport Software Update Manager would apply the "old" Customizing, overwriting any changes on production that are not reflected in the customer transport.

Obviously, one solution is to copy production again to a sandbox system, complete a standard conversion run and afterwards manually execute the FIN&ML conversion activities which result in a new, updated Customizing transport.

☹️This basically means, that any downtime-optimized conversion cycle (e.g. sandbox, load verification, dress rehearsal, go-live) needs a preceding standard conversion run - which massively bloats the project plan. This is a major drawback of the approach as it leads to a longer project runtime and therefore introduces complexity to the planning.

Well, I should say this has been a major drawback...

 

... mitigated with Customizing on Temporary Instance

As the downtime-optimized conversion approach is constantly evolving it also covers a solution for the outdated Customizing issue. With Customizing on Temporary Instance, Software Update Manager offers the possibility to adapt the respective Customizing during the downtime-optimized conversion run itself - eliminating the need for an additional standard cycle. Note that the first standard conversion cycle is still required.

The following screenshot of Software Update Manager shows the dialog where you confirm that the relevant Customizing activities are complete (version 2.0 SP23 PL0):

In this phase, your application experts can log on to the temporary instance (which "reuses" of the shadow instance). The temporary instance exists on the same host with the same SID as the original instance but has a different instance number (the shadow instance number chosen in an earlier Software Update Manager dialog).

Once the Customizing settings have been adjusted, the downtime-optimized conversion run continues. These Customizing settings are saved in a transport which can then be added to the list of customer transports that are provided to the Software Update Manager in the upcoming downtime-optimized conversion cycles - ensuring that the latest changes are reflected. 

 

Additional Resources

To get started with your transition to SAP S/4HANA with this optimized flavor of the system conversion, here's a collection of information:

  • Support Portal Page of Software Update Manager: https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/software-update-manager.html
  • Support Portal Page of downtime-optimized conversion approach of Software Update Manager: https://support.sap.com/en/tools/software-logistics-tools/software-update-manager/downtime-optimized-conversion-approach.html
  • Guide for downtime-optimized Conversion with Software Update Manager: https://help.sap.com/docs/help/c4ebc2b5d928446180d9ad2667f11faa/c603616d71104e36a48330be959ed8dc.html?locale=en-US 
  • Additional information on Customizing on Temporary Instance: SAP Note 3444013 - Executing and Monitoring Downtime-Optimized Conversion to SAP S/4HANA with Customizing On Temporary Instance

Let us know what you think and feel free to comment below!

For any technical issues, please open an SAP case on component BC-UPG-DTM-TLA.

All the best for your project!

entry.title: Re: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Re: How to Find the Table/CDS Views Name by giving field Name in SAP ECC and SAP S/4 HANA
entry.content: I can see fiori APP named as "View Browser" which does the same.

entry.title: Re: Problem in changing Production order components.
entry.content:
This commit code worked.
Thanks, @Arun_Prabhu_K !

entry.title: Re: Major bug in SAP. We do not need SP Key rather we sho...
entry.content:
Hello @PrasannaThawait 

I don't think it is a major bug. It is rather that you don't fully grasp material master data. 

Special procurement indicator (I guess, that's what you mean by SP) doesn't need to be defined for each source and destination plant. It needs to be defined for each source plant only. The indicator defines from which plant materials are procured, not to which plant materials are shipped.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

Ps. Please don't introduce an abbreviation, e.g. SP without explaining it first. That makes your post hard to understand.

entry.title: Re: Stock Transfer Order from 1 to Multiple Plants
entry.content:
Hi Sam,

I'm sorry for the late answer. 

I've created an example for you.

First you go back to the Fiori app“Transfer Stock  - In-Plant“


There, you search for your material and your original plant. For example, material IF11 and plant 1010. In this plant, we have 40,000 pieces of unrestricted-use inventory.

Highlight the line and go to Stock Single Material.

Mark the targets again and go to Transfer Stock- Cross-Plant

Then again on Transfer Stock – Cros-Planet

There you select the corresponding plant of origin and then the plant that is to receive the goods.

The following mask opens. 

You can now add the quantity or, if necessary, the storage location. Post 

Here we see that we have rebooked the 30,000 PCs at Plant 1010 to 1710

 

Make sure beforehand that all master data is maintained in the plants.

 

Best Reagrds 

Dennis

entry.title: Re: 🇮🇳 SAP CodeJam: Connecting systems and services using SAP Integration Suite (Mumbai, India)
entry.content:

Colgate Research Centre,
Main St, Hiranandani Gardens,
Sainath Nagar, Powai, Mumbai,
Maharashtra 400076

https://maps.app.goo.gl/qjSDuDoyZ6uB4cBV6

entry.title: Re: Missing index file at DART/GDPDU extract
entry.content:
Hello dear user,

And thank you for asking your question in the SAP Community blog.

In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, when using scope item 5XU (Document and Reporting Compliance – Reporting: Audit File Extraction), particularly for DART-like extraction (e.g. for Germany or other countries), the index file is not generated by default.

However, the index file (commonly referred to as index.xml or similar) is required by some auditors (especially in Germany, for GDPdU or GoBD audits) as it serves to describe the content and structure of the extract, enabling audit software to process it.

 

Current SAP Behavior (as of 2024/2025):

  • The Run Statutory Reports app with 5XU supports DART-like extracts for audit purposes.
  • The index file is not generated automatically in standard DRC reporting scenarios.
  • As of now, there is no configuration option in the public cloud to enable the generation of the index file directly during extraction.

 

Here’s what you can try to do if you want to get such thing :

Raise an SAP Influence Request or Incident

  • If the auditor mandates the index file, and this is not supported by the current public cloud configuration:
    • You can raise a support incident with SAP referencing the missing index file for audit compliance under the DRC (5XU) process.
    • Alternatively, submit a request via the Customer Influence Portal to add index file generation support in public cloud extractions.

 

I hope this will help.

Best regards,

Jeremy

entry.title: Hi Team,Open GR/IR balance migration tools in S/4 HanaTo create full PO and to poste legacy GR
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Open GR/IR balance migration tools in S/4 Hana
To create full PO and to poste legacy GR without impacting stock entry.

Legacy system : Non SAP system

Example:

PO created in Legacy 100 Qty, 40 QTY delivered But Invoice not posted in Legacy,

As per Migration process we are migrating 60 Qty through, DMC open PO's migration tool.

expecting below process

Step1: Create PO with full quantity

Step2: Migrate PO history ( Legacy GR) without impacting Stock.

Thanks

entry.title: I am simulating a jms queue using 2 iflows in SAP integration suite
entry.content:
I am simulating a jms queue using 2 iflows in SAP integration suite, one will send the data in a xml say ten records, which will have general spiltter that will split into single record and send to jms queue through sender adapter to second iflow, which will process and send the data to  an API http://123.133.1XX.111:5000/create_event through http adapter , I tested that by stopping the http://123.133.1XX.111:5000 server and test whether the data 10 records are there in jms queue  and is retrying.

The HTTP call with request reply is failing as the target system is  down (http://123.133.1XX.111:5000), but the data is not in the jms queue, it is only coming when there is duplicate datastore error or any error in the iflow steps , external call failure of http is not making the 10 records go in jms queue, I am achieving that by forcing  a error from a groovy from the exception sub process, like this
def errorMessage = message.getProperty("CamelExceptionCaught")?.toString()

if (errorMessage?.contains("Connection refused") ||
errorMessage?.contains("Read timed out") ||
errorMessage?.contains("500")) {
message.setProperty("sendToJMS", true);
throw new RuntimeException("Forced retry due to external system down");
} else {
message.setProperty("sendToJMS", false);
}

 

Is this the normal behaviour, please advise.

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: add new items to an existing outbound delivery
entry.content:
Hi Eminr, i have the problem to use the BAPI "BAPI_OUTB_DELIVERY_CHANGE" to add new item to in existing Delivery. Can you please explain your solution in detail?

Thank you and BR

Khoa

entry.title: Unable to format SAP build output coming from LLM
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am working on an copilot created using SAP Build apps which talks with SAP AI core instance to answer queries asked .

The app works perfectly fine but the outcome lacks formats and colors which we usually see when we ask similiar questions to other copilots or chatbots.

Please find attached my apps output and other copilots output .Can you help me how I can retain the format of the outcome of LLM and display it on my application?

SAP Build 

SAP AI Core
